policy_text: All prices in the catalog are in Indian Rupees (INR), exclusive of applicable GST (18% on software and professional services).
Implementation CapEx pricing is a one-time, milestone-linked cost of INR 312 Crores in Year 0.

policy_text: Annual Managed Services (AMS) OpEx in Year 1 is INR 68 Crores, commencing from Phase 3 go-live.

policy_text: AMS OpEx in Year 2 includes a 4% escalation over Year 1, totaling INR 72 Crores.

policy_text: AMS OpEx in Year 3 includes a 4% escalation over Year 2, totaling INR 76 Crores.

policy_text: The catalog is contractually anchored as the baseline pricing structure for Tier-1 telecom BSS/OSS transformation engagements.
source_section: Five-Year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Summary

policy_text: The catalog covers Implementation CapEx pricing by workstream, Annual Managed Services (AMS) OpEx by service tier, software license structures, optional modules, volume-based pricing tiers, and payment milestone schedules.
Year 4 operational expenditure (OpEx) is INR 79 Crores with a 4% annual escalation rate from Year 3.
Year 5 operational expenditure (OpEx) is INR 83 Crores with a 4% annual escalation rate from Year 4.
The 5-Year Total Cost of Ownership is INR 690 Crores, excluding optional modules and including cloud IaaS pass-through at cost.
Year 0 implementation capital expenditure (CapEx) is INR 312 Crores, structured across five delivery workstreams as defined in the RFP scope of work.
Stream A (Platform Design & Architecture) has an indicative cost of INR 22 Crores and a duration of 8 weeks (Phase 0).
Stream B (Software Deployment & Configuration) spans 34 weeks (Phase 1–2B) and includes deployment of all BSS/OSS modules, 40+ integrations, and performance/security testing.
Stream C (Data Migration) spans 28 weeks (Phase 2B–3) and involves migrating 215M subscriber records, 24-month CDR history, and ensuring zero-revenue-leakage cutover.
Stream D (Training & Change Management) includes training for 200 super-users, 3,500+ operations staff, and delivery of runbooks and documentation.
The cloud IaaS pass-through cost is included in the 5-Year Total Cost of Ownership at actual cost, with no markup applied.
The implementation workstreams must adhere to the defined phase durations: 8 weeks for Phase 0, 34 weeks for Phases 1–2B, and 28 weeks for Phases 2B–3.
The data migration process must ensure zero-revenue-leakage during the cutover of 215M subscriber records and 24-month CDR history.
The platform design and architecture workstream includes As-Is assessment, target architecture, cloud design, and data migration strategy.
The software deployment and configuration workstream includes performance and security testing for all BSS/OSS modules and 40+ integrations.
Post-Go-Live Hypercare includes 90-day war-room support per phase with a 24x7 dedicated team
Hypercare support is delivered phase-by-phase for 90 days each
34 Cr is allocated for Post-Go-Live Hypercare and included in Year 0 budget
Project Management & Governance requires a 150-person programme team including PMO, architecture board, and QA governance
Total Year 0 Implementation CapEx is INR 312 Crores over ~18 months
Critical Systems (OCS, Charging, Fraud) service tier includes L2/L3 Incident Management + Proactive Monitoring
Billing & Revenue Assurance service tier includes L2/L3 Incident + Billing cycle support
CRM & Order Management L2 Incident + Configuration changes are handled during business hours (8AM–8PM IST Mon–Sat).
Self-Care & Digital Channels L2 Incident + Content updates are handled during business hours (Mon–Fri).
Cloud Infrastructure (AWS) IaaS management, cost optimization, and patching are provided 24x7 automated and business hours manual.
Two platform enhancement releases per year are included as part of the standard service.
Major Enhancements (T&M) beyond the 2 base releases or new module development are quoted separately and billed as time-and-material.
24x7x365 availability is required during bill run periods, with business hours support otherwise.
source_section: 24x7x365 during bill run; business hours otherwise

policy_text: The annual fee for Year 1 (FY2027–28) is INR 68 Cr, serving as the base year.
The annual fee for Year 2 (FY2028–29) is INR 72 Cr, with a 4% escalation of INR 2.72 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 3 (FY2029–30) is INR 76 Cr, with a 4% escalation of INR 2.88 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 4 (FY2030–31) is INR 79 Cr, with a 4% escalation of INR 3.04 Cr.
The annual fee for Year 5 (FY2031–32) is INR 83 Cr, with a 4% escalation of INR 3.16 Cr.
The total cumulative OpEx over FY2027–32 is INR 378 Crores.
The AMS escalation is contractually capped at 4% per year, which is the lower of 4% or the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Government of India.
The RFP requirement of a 5%/WPI cap is fulfilled by the 4% annual escalation cap.
The extended warranty (Years 6–7) has a flat annual maintenance cost of INR 41 Cr with no escalation.
The extended warranty (Years 6–7) must be exercised 6 months before Year 5 expiry.
NexWave Technologies must not deploy end-of-life billing platforms for clients.
NexWave Technologies must ensure zero billing disruption during platform migration for clients.
BSS/OSS transformation projects must be completed within a 22-month migration duration for large-scale deployments.
NexWave Technologies must obtain client CIO certification for zero billing disruption in migration projects.
NexWave Technologies must replace legacy platforms that lack convergent billing capabilities for prepaid and postpaid services.
Order fulfillment cycles must not exceed 96 hours for BSS/OSS platforms to prevent subscriber churn.
NexWave Technologies must deploy BSS/OSS solutions capable of supporting convergent billing for prepaid and postpaid subscribers.
NexWave Technologies must implement BSS/OSS platforms with 4G and 5G slicing capabilities.
NexWave Technologies must enforce zero tolerance for billing disruptions during platform migration projects.
NexWave Technologies must execute greenfield BSS/OSS transformation projects for clients requiring full platform replacement.
NexWave Technologies must ensure contract values for BSS/OSS transformations are comparable to Tier-1 scale engagements.
NexWave Technologies must deliver BSS/OSS go-live dates within the agreed timeline (e.g., December 2022 for AsiaNet Wireless).
NexWave Technologies must migrate at least 120 million subscribers in large-scale BSS/OSS transformation projects.
NexWave Technologies must replace legacy platforms with limited 4G capabilities to avoid subscriber inconsistencies.
NexWave Technologies must prioritize platform replacement for clients with end-of-life legacy systems.
TeleSphere OCS v7.1 supports 3GPP Rel-16 Ro/Gy/Rf convergent charging for 120M subscribers.
TeleSphere Billing Engine generates convergent bills for postpaid and prepaid subscribers.
TeleSphere CRM provides a 360-degree customer view and replaces legacy Amdocs CRM.
TeleSphere OMS uses BPMN-based order orchestration with 45 pre-built order workflow types.
TeleSphere NIM eliminates shadow inventory across 180,000 sites.
TeleSphere DataBridge includes pre-built Ericsson BSCS certified extraction and transformation connectors.
Cloud deployment is active-active in AWS ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) and ap-southeast-3 (Jakarta).
NexWave deployed the TeleSphere Migration Factory framework: Extract → Cleanse → Transform → Load → Validate → Reconcile → Cutover.
Subscriber migration occurred in two phases: enterprise and postpaid (12M) in Phase A, followed by prepaid (108M) in Phase B.
A mandatory 60-day parallel run was executed for both migration phases.
Both legacy and new systems ran simultaneously with real-time reconciliation dashboards monitored 24x7 by a dedicated war-room team in Manila and Bengaluru.
Zero billing disruption was achieved with 100% subscriber records migrated and no billing errors.
OCS rating latency must be <41ms P99 at 1.5M TPS post go-live.
Order fulfillment cycles were reduced from 96 hours to <3.8 hours average (96% improvement).
No-touch order rate increased to 94.7% (up from 28% on legacy OMS).
Shadow inventory was eliminated with 6.2M Excel-based records decommissioned.
Programme duration was 22 months (Phase A: 12 months; Phase B: 10 months).
Client sign-off was provided by Maria Santos, Head of IT, AsiaNet Wireless.

This engagement is the closest direct reference to Vi's requirements for greenfield BSS/OSS, migration from Ericsson BSCS, 100M+ subscriber scale, zero-billing-disruption constraint, and convergent charging requirements.
Stellar Mobile in Indonesia engaged NexWave for a full BSS transformation with a focus on prepaid.
Stellar Mobile has 280 million subscribers, representing the largest NexWave deployment.
The legacy platform uses Huawei SDP for prepaid and Amdocs Ensemble for postpaid, preventing convergent charging.
The legacy platform caused revenue leakage estimated at 2.3% of annual revenue (~$340M).
The legacy platform could not scale beyond 800K TPS for real-time charging, causing peak-hour rate limiting.
The legacy platform blocked the client's 5G rollout pending OCS readiness for 5G SA Nchf interface.
The OCS TPS sustained 1.5M TPS in production and tested up to 2M TPS.
The OCS Go-Live date is October 2023.
OCS rating latency must be <38ms at 1.5M TPS (production-proven).
OCS availability must be 99.9997% (12-month post-go-live average).
NexWave deployed TeleSphere OCS v7.2 with 3GPP Rel-17 Ro/Gy/Rf and 5G SA Nchf interfaces.
NexWave deployed TeleSphere Billing Engine for convergent bill generation for 280M subscribers.
NexWave deployed TeleSphere Revenue Assurance with Apache Flink for real-time stream processing.
NexWave deployed TeleSphere Fraud Management with LSTM-based ML model for SIM clone detection.
The solution uses AWS ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) active-active with auto-scaling for peak traffic.
OCS rating latency P99 must be <38ms at 1.5M TPS (exceeds Vi requirement of <50ms).
Peak TPS tested must be 2M TPS, with 1.5M TPS production SLA contractually guaranteed.
OCS availability must be 99.9997% (exceeds five-nines target).
SIM clone alert-to-block time must be less than 3 minutes
False positive rate for SIM clone detection must not exceed 0.4%
5G SA Nchf interface is live and operational
Network slice usage-based charging is operational
NexWave's production results exceed Vi OCS SLA thresholds of <50ms P99 at 1.5M TPS and 99.999% availability
5G slice lifecycle management includes design, order, instantiate, assure, modify, and terminate

All externally-facing proposal documents, RFP responses, marketing collateral, and client communications must comply with the NexWave Technologies India Brand Guide.
The tagline 'Powering India's Telecom Future' must be used on all India-market materials including proposal covers, executive summaries, and marketing decks.
NexWave communications must consistently project the brand personality of Expert, Reliable, Innovative, and Committed.
The full legal name 'NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd.' must be used in all external documents, with the short form 'NexWave' allowed only after the first mention.
The product name 'TeleSphere BSS/OSS Suite' must be used in all external references to the company's product.
The RFP automation system must be used to ensure generated proposals comply with brand standards.
NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. is certified as India's leading BSS/OSS platform company, serving 28 mobile operators across 18 countries.
NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. enables telecom operators to power their digital transformation through real-time convergent charging and 5G network slice monetization.
The product name 'TeleSphere' must never be abbreviated as 'NWT' or 'NWTECH' in client documents.
The registered office address for proposals is '12th Floor, Embassy TechSquare, Outer Ring Road, Bengaluru – 560103'.
The Company Identification Number (CIN) 'U72200KA2009PTC123456' must only be used in legal documents.
The official website URL is 'www.nexwavetech.in'.
The primary brand color 'NexWave Red' (#E60000) must be used for primary brand elements, headings, and call-to-action (CTA) buttons.
The primary brand color 'NexWave Navy' (#1A1A2E) must be used for body text and dark backgrounds.
The primary brand color 'Pure White' (#FFFFFF) must be used for page backgrounds and clean spaces.
The secondary brand color 'Light Gray' (#F5F5F5) must be used for table alternating rows and callouts.
The secondary brand color 'Mid Gray' (#888888) must be used for footer text, captions, and metadata.
Never use more than 3 colours on a single page.
Red is reserved for NexWave branding — do not use for warnings unless combined with text.
Maintain WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios for all text: minimum 4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text.
Document Title (cover) must use Arial font, 52pt / Bold, NexWave Navy colour.
Sub-Title (cover) must use Arial font, 26pt / Regular, NexWave Red colour.
Heading 1 must use Arial font, 28pt / Bold, NexWave Navy colour with red bottom border.
Primary font for client-facing documents must be Arial.
Calibri, Times New Roman, or decorative fonts are prohibited in client-facing documents.
Fallback font for client-facing documents must be Helvetica Neue.
Page size for India market documents must be A4 (210mm x 297mm), not US Letter.
All proposal documents must use 1-inch (72pt / 1440 DXA) margins on all sides.
Content width for A4 documents with 1-inch margins must be approximately 9,026 DXA (6.27 inches).
Body text in documents must use single line spacing with 8pt spacing before and after paragraphs.
Body text must be left-aligned; cover titles must be center-aligned.
Table cell padding must be Top: 80–100 DXA, Bottom: 80–100 DXA, Left: 120 DXA, Right: 120 DXA.
Every page must include a header with 'Company name | Document title | Classification level'.
Every page must include a footer with 'Document reference | Version | Classification | Page number'.
Client proposals must include the cover page elements in the specified order.
Document title must be 52pt Bold, NexWave Navy.
Sub-title must be 26pt, NexWave Red, with a red bottom border line beneath it.
Prepared by / Submitted to lines must be 20pt, Mid Gray.
Document reference, version, date, and classification must be 18pt, Mid Gray.
Classification banner (CONFIDENTIAL / INTERNAL USE ONLY) must be 18pt Bold, NexWave Red.
Table header row must have NexWave Navy background (#1A1A2E), white text, and 18pt Bold font.
Alternating row shading must use White (#FFFFFF) and Light Gray (#F5F5F5) with ShadingType.CLEAR.
All table borders must be single line, 1pt, light gray (#DDDDDD).
Highlight/totals rows must use Light Red Tint (#FFF0F0) with bold text.
Tables must specify both columnWidths (array) and individual cell widths (DXA) for consistent rendering.
Percentage widths must not be used; only DXA values are allowed for cross-platform compatibility.
All RFP responses must include the 'Executive Summary' section with 2 pages max, Vi-specific content, and mirrored RFP language with quantified outcomes.

Payment terms for India proposals: Net 30 days from invoice with milestone-linked structure and bank guarantee for advance payment.

Include at least 3 client references with contact details and note availability for site visits.
Use the TeleSphere Migration Factory for data migration with a zero-leakage commitment and parallel run plan.
Define a phase plan, named team, milestone commitments, and critical blackout periods in implementation methodology.
Include SLA schedule, hypercare plan, and 5-year AMS structure in support and AMS model.
Reference financials, certifications, and references from document NWTECH-CP-2026-001 in company profile.
Provide a compliance matrix per RFP section in technical solution.
Structure commercial proposal with milestone payments and TCO summary from document NWTECH-PPC-2026-001.

policy_text: NexWave Technologies implements Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) with Privileged Access Management (PAM) integration.
CyberArk Privileged Access Management (PAM) is used for privileged access control.
Open Policy Agent (OPA) policy engine is used for Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC).
NexWave Technologies has integrated a GSMA FS.11/FS.19-compliant signaling firewall certified as P1 Security.
The GSMA FS.11/FS.19 signaling firewall is deployed at all 5 Tier-1 reference sites.
Immutable audit logs with 36-month retention are implemented using AWS CloudTrail, Splunk SIEM, and S3 Object Lock (WORM).
NexWave Technologies' Bengaluru delivery center is ISO 27001:2022 certified by Bureau Veritas.
The ISO 27001:2022 certification for the Bengaluru delivery center was last audited in February 2025.
The company holds a valid PCI-DSS v4.0 Level 1 certification issued by KPMG India, valid until December 2026.
Bi-annual external penetration testing is conducted by Mandiant and KPMG, with critical findings remediated within 30 days.
The company is empanelled by MeitY for cloud services via AWS India regions ap-south-1 (Mumbai) and ap-south-2 (Hyderabad).
All data is stored exclusively in AWS ap-south-1/ap-south-2 regions with no cross-border transfers enforced by ILM policies.
The company complies with the DPDP Act 2023 through a ready DPA template, PII tokenization, and a consent management module.
An active-active multi-region disaster recovery system is maintained with RPO <15 minutes and RTO <1 hour, tested every 6 months.

NexWave acknowledges and accepts obligations as a 'Data Processor' under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 for all subscriber personal data processed on behalf of Vi (the 'Data Fiduciary').
Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is pre-drafted and aligned to DPDP Act Sections 8 and 9, ready for execution at contract signing.
All MSISDN, Aadhaar-linked identifiers, and payment data are tokenized at the point of ingestion using AWS Tokenization Service; raw PII is never stored in application databases.
TeleSphere Consent Module maintains granular per-subscriber consent records for marketing communications, data sharing, and profiling with audit trails maintained for 3 years.
Zero subscriber data leaves India territory, confirmed by AWS data residency attestation and network egress monitoring.
Automated ILM policies enforce TRAI-mandated CDR retention (2 years active; 5 years archive) and subscriber deletion workflows compliant with DPDP erasure rights.
NexWave commits to notifying Vi within 72 hours of a confirmed personal data breach, as required by DPDP Act Section 8(6).
Automated TRAI QoS report generation via TeleSphere Regulatory Reports module is live at BrightPath Mobile (India) with no manual intervention.
TeleSphere supports DOT-mandated Lawful Intercept and Monitoring (LIM) requirements with CALEA-compatible architecture.
TAP/NRTRDE interfaces for international roaming compliance and BARG/TADIG compliance are implemented.
Automated reconciliation ensures TRAI revenue figures match internal billing records within 0.01% tolerance.
NexWave is a PCI-DSS Level 1 Service Provider, the highest tier of PCI compliance for entities processing >6 million card transactions per year.
Cardholder data environment (CDE) is fully isolated from non-payment systems via network segmentation and dedicated key management.
Payment gateway integrations (NPCI/UPI, Razorpay, Paytm) are pre-certified; no new cardholder data flows are introduced without PCI change control.
Annual on-site audit by QSA (KPMG India), quarterly internal vulnerability scans, and bi-annual external penetration testing are conducted.
AWS India regions (ap-south-1 Mumbai, ap-south-2 Hyderabad) are certified with ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II compliance.
All data stored in ap-south-1 (Mumbai) and ap-south-2 (Hyderabad); no cross-border transfers; contractually guaranteed
AWS KMS + CloudHSM used for key management
FIPS 140-2 Level 3 HSM is used for key management
Customer-managed keys (CMK) option is available for Vi
NexWave SOC (Bengaluru) responds to P1 security events within 15 minutes
Initial containment of P1 security events must occur within 1 hour
Critical (CVSS 9.0+) pen test findings must be remediated within 30 days of identification
High-severity pen test findings must be remediated within 60 days of identification
Personal data breach must be notified to Vi within 72 hours
Personal data breach must be notified to the Data Protection Board of India as required by DPDP Act
NexWave operates a responsible disclosure program for vulnerability tracking
Recovery Point Objective (RPO) must be less than 15 minutes
Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be less than 1 hour
Planned DR failover tests must be conducted every 6 months
24x7 Hypercare War Room must be maintained for 90 days post each phase go-live
Professional Indemnity insurance coverage must be INR 100 Crores per claim
Professional Indemnity insurance must be provided by New India Assurance Co.
Professional Indemnity insurance policy period must cover FY2026-27 and be renewable annually
The Standard Terms & Conditions govern the relationship between NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd. ('Vendor') and the Client for the delivery of BSS/OSS platform modernization services.
The Terms are incorporated by reference into the Master Services Agreement ('MSA') and all associated Statements of Work ('SOW').
In case of conflict between the Terms and an executed MSA, the MSA prevails.
The Master Services Agreement ('MSA') includes these Terms, all SOWs, SLA schedules, pricing exhibits, and data processing agreements.
Vendor outputs include software modules, configurations, documents, reports, data migration artifacts, or other outputs specified in the applicable SOW.
Services include implementation, software deployment, data migration, training, hypercare support, and Application Managed Services (AMS) as defined in the relevant SOW.

The TeleSphere Suite is NexWave's proprietary BSS/OSS software platform, including modules like OCS, Billing Engine, CRM, OMS, NIM, Revenue Assurance, Fraud Management, Self-Care, and SliceOrchestrator.
Subscriber Data includes all personal and non-personal data relating to the Client's subscribers, such as MSISDN records, CDRs, billing history, consent records, and CRM data.
A platform phase is accepted by the Client as production-ready only after successful completion of acceptance testing criteria defined in the SOW.
The AMS Commencement Date is the Phase 3 Go-Live Date as specified in the commercial exhibit.
Modifications to SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms must be formalized through the Change Control Process defined in Clause 7.
The Vendor must mobilize the programme team as specified in the SOW, including named key personnel (Programme Director, Architecture Lead, and Domain Leads), from the contract effective date.
Named key personnel shall not be replaced without the Client's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
In the event of an unavoidable replacement of named key personnel, the Vendor must provide a candidate with equivalent or superior qualifications for Client approval within 15 business days.
The Vendor must commit to the milestone schedule defined in the applicable SOW.
The Vendor must notify the Client in writing at least 20 business days in advance if a milestone will not be met.
Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones trigger delay credits defined in Schedule 2 (SLA & Service Credits).
For subscriber data migration, the Vendor guarantees zero billing disruption and zero revenue leakage as defined in the Migration Acceptance Criteria (Appendix to SOW).
Data migration acceptance requires 100% subscriber record count reconciliation.
Data migration acceptance requires CDR value reconciliation within 0.01% tolerance.
Data migration acceptance requires parallel run sign-off by the Client's billing team.
The Vendor must remedy billing errors attributable to migration activities within 90 days of Go-Live at no additional cost.
The platform must meet or exceed performance benchmarks specified in Schedule 1 (SLA Schedule) from the Go-Live Date.
Performance benchmarks include OCS rating latency (<50ms P99 at 1.5M TPS), availability (99.999% for OCS/Charging), and bill run completion (10M accounts in <6 hours).
Failure to meet performance benchmarks triggers service credits per Schedule 2.
The Client shall provide timely access to legacy system data, APIs, and documentation necessary for migration and integration.
The Client shall make available Subject Matter Experts for workshops, testing, and UAT sign-off within agreed timelines.
The Client shall process Change Requests within 10 business days.
The Client shall process invoices within Net 30 days.
The Client shall designate a named Programme Sponsor and Project Manager with authority to approve deliverables.
The Client shall provide access to Vi's data centers and network environments required for on-premises integration points.
The TeleSphere BSS/OSS Suite is and shall remain the exclusive intellectual property of NexWave Technologies Pvt. Ltd.
The Client is granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-included license to use the TeleSphere Suite solely for its internal business operations as part of the contracted Services.
The Client may not sublicense, resell, or distribute the TeleSphere Suite.
The Client may not reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the TeleSphere Suite.
The Client may not create derivative works based on the TeleSphere Suite.
All Subscriber Data, Client configuration data, business rules, and proprietary processes provided by the Client remain the exclusive property of the Client.
The Vendor shall use Subscriber Data solely for the purpose of performing the contracted Services.

On termination or expiry of the Agreement, the Vendor shall return or destroy all Subscriber Data within 60 days, as directed by the Client, subject to any retention requirements under applicable law.

Custom software, configurations, and integration adapters developed specifically for the Client as part of the agreed SOW ('Bespoke Deliverables') shall be owned jointly by both parties unless otherwise specified in the SOW.
The Vendor retains the right to use the methodologies and know-how embodied in Bespoke Deliverables in future engagements, provided no Client-confidential information is disclosed.
Both parties shall maintain strict confidentiality of the other's Confidential Information for the duration of the Agreement and for 3 years after termination.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that is in the public domain at the time of disclosure.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information independently developed by the receiving party.
Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information required to be disclosed by law or regulatory order.
The Client may share the Vendor's commercial terms with its board and advisors under equivalent confidentiality obligations.
Implementation payments are milestone-linked as specified in the Commercial Exhibit.
AMS payments are monthly in arrears from the AMS Commencement Date.
All amounts are exclusive of applicable GST (currently 18% on software and professional services).
Invoices shall be raised by the Vendor within 5 business days of milestone achievement, accompanied by evidence of completion.
Client shall pay undisputed invoices within Net 30 days of receipt.
For disputed invoices, the Client shall raise a written dispute within 15 business days with specific grounds.
Both parties shall resolve disputes within 30 days of the dispute notice.
Undisputed portions of disputed invoices shall be paid within the original Net 30 timeline.
Overdue amounts beyond 45 days shall accrue interest at SBI MCLR (1-year) + 2% per annum, calculated daily.
Persistent non-payment beyond 90 days (with a 30-day cure notice) shall entitle the Vendor to suspend non-critical services upon written notice.
AMS fees shall escalate annually at the lower of: (a) 4% per year (contractually committed), or (b) the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) published by the Ministry of Commerce, Government of India.
This cap is a contractual commitment and cannot be exceeded.
Escalation applies from the second AMS year onward.
All changes to agreed SOW scope, timeline, or commercial terms shall be governed by this Change Control Process.
Either party may raise a Change Request ('CR') in writing using the standard CR form.
The Vendor shall provide a commercial and timeline impact assessment within 10 business days.
The Client shall approve, reject, or counter-propose within 10 business days of receiving the impact assessment.
No change shall be implemented without a written Change Order signed by both parties' authorized representatives.
CRs that reduce scope shall not entitle the Vendor to additional payment, but may reduce milestone-linked payments proportionally as agreed.
The parties acknowledge that service levels are material terms of the Agreement.
The SLA schedule (Schedule 1) defines availability, latency, response, and resolution targets for each system component.
SLA measurement periods, exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure, Client-caused outages), and credit calculation methodologies are defined in Schedule 1.
OCS / Charging Engine has an SLA target of 99.999% availability.
Credit trigger for OCS / Charging Engine is <99.999% availability in a calendar month.
Monthly credit rate for OCS / Charging Engine is 2% of monthly AMS fee per 0.001% shortfall.
CRM / Order Management must maintain 99.99% availability in a calendar month.
If CRM / Order Management availability falls below 99.99% in a calendar month, a service credit of 1% of monthly AMS fee is applied per 0.01% shortfall.
Self-Care Portal must maintain 99.95% availability in a calendar month.
If Self-Care Portal availability falls below 99.95% in a calendar month, a service credit of 0.5% of monthly AMS fee is applied per 0.05% shortfall.
OCS Rating Latency P99 must remain below 50ms at 1.5M TPS.
If OCS Rating Latency P99 exceeds 50ms sustained for >30 minutes, a service credit of 1% of monthly AMS fee is applied per incident.
Critical Incident Response must occur within 15 minutes (P1).
If Critical Incident Response exceeds 15 minutes (P1), a service credit of 0.5% of monthly AMS fee is applied per breach.
Total service credits are capped at a maximum of 15% of monthly AMS fee in any month.
Unexcused delays beyond 30 days on critical path milestones trigger liquidated damages (LD) at 0.5% of total implementation CapEx per week of delay.
Liquidated damages for implementation delays are capped at a maximum of 5% of total implementation CapEx.
Liquidated damages are the Client's sole remedy for implementation delays and cannot be combined with service credits.
Liquidated damages do not apply if delays are caused by Client actions (e.g., delayed approvals, access, or data provision) or force majeure events.

Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of revenue, profits, data, or business opportunity, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.

The Vendor's total aggregate liability under the Agreement shall not exceed the greater of: (a) the total fees paid by the Client in the 12 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or (b) INR 100 Crores.

Liability limitations do not apply to: (i) death or personal injury caused by negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) breaches of confidentiality or data protection obligations; or (iv) indemnification obligations under Clause 10.

The Vendor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Client against third-party claims arising from: (a) infringement of third-party intellectual property rights by the TeleSphere Suite; (b) gross negligence or willful misconduct of Vendor personnel; (c) data breaches directly caused by Vendor's failure to maintain required security controls.

The Client shall indemnify the Vendor against claims arising from: (a) Client's misuse of the platform contrary to agreed specifications; (b) Client's failure to comply with applicable telecom regulations; or (c) Client-provided content or data that infringes third-party rights.

Either party may terminate the Agreement by written notice if the other party: (a) commits a material breach and fails to remedy it within 60 days of written notice; (b) becomes insolvent, enters liquidation, or is subject to a winding-up order; or (c) engages in corrupt practices or violates applicable anti-bribery laws.
In case of Client termination for Vendor's cause, the Vendor shall refund all milestone payments for undelivered milestones within 90 days.
The Client may terminate the AMS contract for convenience with 6 months' written notice after Year 2 of the AMS term.

Termination for convenience during the implementation phase requires payment of: (a) all completed milestone payments; (b) reasonable mobilization costs for work-in-progress (not to exceed 20% of remaining implementation CapEx); and (c) committed third-party costs that cannot be cancelled.
The Vendor shall not be entitled to anticipated profits on unperformed work.

On termination or expiry, the Vendor shall provide exit assistance for up to 12 months, including: knowledge transfer to the Client's designated successor vendor, data extraction in standard formats (CSV/JSON), platform documentation, and API specifications.
Exit assistance is charged at the T&M rates defined in the Commercial Exhibit.
The Vendor's obligation to maintain the platform continues during the exit assistance period.

Dispute resolution steps: (1) Escalation via project representatives within 15 business days; (2) Senior management (CXO level) for 30-day resolution attempt; (3) Mediation via ICADR or DIAC within 45 days; (4) Arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, with Mumbai as the seat of arbitration.
Arbitration shall be conducted in English with 1 (sole arbitrator by mutual agreement) or 3 arbitrators (if parties cannot agree within 30 days).
Arbitration award shall be final and binding.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
Jurisdiction for interim relief: Courts of competent jurisdiction at Mumbai, Maharashtra.
Cloud-Native: All services containerized (Docker/Helm); deployed on Kubernetes (EKS); infrastructure-as-code via Terraform
Event-Driven Microservices: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) architecture; Apache Kafka v3.6 as central event bus (800K+ events/second capacity)
API-First: TM Forum Open API-compliant REST and AsyncAPI
All services expose TM Forum-standard APIs natively
Active-Active Multi-Region: Mumbai primary + Hyderabad DR; automated geo-failover; no single point of failure
Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) for Kubernetes
Tested to 300M subscribers without re-architecture
Prometheus + Grafana (metrics) for observability
ELK stack (log aggregation) for observability
Jaeger (distributed tracing) for observability
Splunk SIEM (security) for observability
TeleSphere OCS version 7.2
Compliance with 3GPP Rel-17 Ro/Gy/Rf + Nchf standards
Production scale of 280M subscribers for TeleSphere OCS
Production scale of 1.5M TPS (transactions per second) for TeleSphere OCS
TeleSphere CRM must comply with TMF632 standard.
TeleSphere CRM must use a SID-aligned data model.
TeleSphere CRM must manage 120M subscribers in AsiaNet Wireless.
TeleSphere OMS must comply with TMF622 standard.
TeleSphere OMS must use BPMN (Camunda) workflow standard.
TeleSphere OMS must achieve 94.7% no-touch automation for AsiaNet.
TeleSphere NIM must comply with TMF637 standard.
TeleSphere NIM must use NETCONF/YANG protocol standards.
TeleSphere NIM must manage 470,000+ nodes.
TeleSphere RA/FM must use Apache Flink for real-time processing.
TeleSphere RA/FM must employ LSTM ML models for analytics.
TeleSphere RA/FM must detect leakage in <30 minutes.
TeleSphere RA/FM must block fraud in <3 minutes.
TeleSphere Self-Care must comply with WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standard.
TeleSphere Self-Care must support PWA + iOS/Android platforms.
TeleSphere Self-Care must achieve 97.2% NLP chatbot intent accuracy.
TeleSphere SliceOrchestrator must comply with 3GPP TS 28.530 standard.
TeleSphere SliceOrchestrator must comply with GSMA NG.116 standard.
TeleSphere SliceOrchestrator must be live at Crescent Telecom for 5G SA (Q1 2024).
TeleSphere CRM must process 280M accounts and 10M bill runs in 4.2 hours.
TeleSphere CRM must comply with TMF676 standard.
TeleSphere CRM must support GST e-invoicing compliance.
The Online Charging System (OCS) natively supports all 3GPP Release 17 Ro/Gy/Rf interfaces for online charging, policy & charging, and offline CDR.
The OCS supports the 5G SA Nchf interface for network slice usage-based charging, which is live in production at Crescent Telecom (Q1 2024).
The OCS achieves a P99 rating latency of <38ms at 1.5M transactions per second (TPS), meeting the <50ms threshold requirement.
The OCS guarantees a peak throughput of 1.5M transactions per second (TPS) in production, with 2M TPS load-tested as contractual assurance.
The OCS scales to support up to 300M subscribers, with 280M live at Stellar Mobile and horizontal scaling tested to 300M without re-architecture.
The OCS enables real-time prepaid balance deduction, reservation, and reauthorization with <38ms P99 latency.
Postpaid convergent CDR-based billing must be compliant with CDR-based mediation and convergent bill generation for postpaid and prepaid unified engine.
Enterprise contract billing must be compliant with contract billing, CPQ integration, volume discounts, and SLA-based credits for enterprise accounts.
OCS Availability must maintain 99.999% uptime (Five-Nines) as a contractual commitment.
The primary data store for OCS balance management must use Apache Cassandra (distributed NoSQL) with sub-millisecond read/write P99 performance.
Session state management must use Redis Cluster for real-time session reservation and balance lock management.
CDR mediation must follow the architecture: Apache Kafka → custom mediation engine → PostgreSQL for billing input.
OCS and Billing must not use separate data stores to avoid OCS billing back-pressure during peak charging periods.
CRM must support unified customer segments: consumer prepaid, postpaid, SME, and enterprise within a single CRM system.
CRM must provide a 360-degree view of omnichannel interaction history (call center, web, app, retail POS) with a unified timeline.
CRM screen load time must achieve <1.4 seconds P99 in production (RFP target: <2 seconds).
CRM must include AI agent assist with real-time sentiment analysis, suggested responses, and automated case routing via the TeleSphere AI engine.
CRM must implement ML-based churn prediction with 30-day propensity scores and next-best-action recommendations.
Legacy migration uses a Salesforce-compatible data model.
Legacy migration includes a Siebel v8.x bidirectional sync adapter.
Legacy migration provides history migration tooling.
TM Forum compliance requires a TM Forum SID-aligned data model.
TM Forum compliance requires the TMF632 Party Management API to be natively exposed.
The Order Management System (OMS) architecture uses microservices.
The OMS workflow engine is BPMN-based (Camunda v7).
The OMS uses event-driven order orchestration.
The OMS provides 45+ pre-built workflows for order types including SIM swap, plan change, number porting, enterprise provisioning, and 5G slice ordering.
No-touch automation must achieve 94.7% order completion (AsiaNet Wireless Q1 2025).
No-touch automation must exceed 90% order completion (RFP target).
SIM swap/plan change fulfillment must have an average of <2 hours (compliant with 50% headroom).
SIM swap/plan change fulfillment must meet an RFP target of <4 hours.
New connection fulfillment must be completed in <4 hours, including SIM provisioning and network activation.
Enterprise order fulfillment (complex CPQ + contract billing) must be completed in <8 hours.
The OMS must natively expose the TMF622 Product Order Management API.
The system provides real-time stream processing using Apache Flink with sub-30-minute leakage detection.
OCS events, mediation records, billing invoices, and network CDRs are reconciled in real-time.
Fraud detection uses a hybrid rule-based and ML model ensemble with LSTM-based anomaly detection.
SIM Clone Alert-to-Block must be achieved in <3 minutes in production with 40% headroom compliance.
ML model retraining occurs daily with a false positive rate <0.5% in production.
A pre-built migration toolkit is provided for SUBEX RAG migration with RA history preservation.
Automated TRAI regulatory reports for QoS and revenue generation require no manual intervention.
Network Inventory Management supports Ericsson, Nokia, Huawei, and ZTE with pre-built adapters.
Discovery protocols include SNMP v2c/v3, NETCONF/YANG, TR-069, SSH, and REST APIs, all natively supported.
Topology views for physical, logical, and service layers with live operational status are provided.
Shadow inventory elimination is automated, decommissioning Excel-based systems within 90 days of go-live.
TMF637 Product Inventory Management API is natively exposed for real-time inventory queries.
No manual data re-entry is required during SUBEX RAG migration.
TRAI regulatory report generation requires no manual intervention.
Daily automated ML model retraining is required to maintain a false positive rate <0.5%.
